Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Answer To The Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Judy Lam, Esq. (310-596-4388; JLam@maynardcooper.com) of Maynard, Cooper & Gale, LLP for Plaintiff, This is Cala, Inc.
James Patrick, Esq. (415-362-2580; James.Patrick@lewisbrisbois.com) of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP for Defendant, Simpler Postage, Inc.
Scott Kessler, Esq. (Scott.Kessler@akerman.com) of Akerman LLP for Defendant, Ruby Has.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Simpler Postage, Inc.'s motion for leave to file a first amended answer to the complaint is granted. California courts liberally allow pleadings amendments at any stage of the proceedings. (Royal Thrift & Loan Co. v. County Escrow, Inc. (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 24, 32; Wrightson v. Dougherty (1936) 5 Cal.2d 257, 265-66 (amendment to answer).) Trial is still more than eight months off.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Eric Throne, CSR No. 7855, Email: eric.throne.csr@gmail.com, Tel: 707-305-8964. =(302/RBU)
